feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Long Island nurse who made $1.5M selling fake COVID vaccine cards slapped with $544K fine
entry.summary: “This is the largest civil penalty imposed for vaccination fraud in the Department of Health’s 125-year history,” State Health Commissioner Dr. James McDonald said of the massive $544,000 fin…

entry.title: Parasitic stomach illness that can cause explosive diarrhea rises in Louisiana
entry.summary: Louisiana health officials are tracking 23 cases of cyclosporiasis, a foodborne parasitic illness that can cause weeks of watery diarrhea.

entry.title: In Congo, health workers fighting Ebola go on strike after months without pay
entry.summary: Doctors and Nurses on the front lines of the Democratic Republic of Congo’s Ebola response have reached a breaking point.

entry.title: Nearly 300 cases of cyclosporiasis reported in NYC, additional 121 across state, health officials say
entry.summary: Nearly 300 cases of cyclosporiasis​ have been reported in New York City alone over the past two months, health officials said Wednesday.

entry.title: Learning What Substance Is Suspected of Causing Alzheimer's May Throw You Into an Existential Crisis
entry.summary: A surprisingly common substance was found to have a causal relationship with the kind of brain damage associated with Alzheimer's disease.

entry.title: Brits' Plan in 1788 to Stop Smallpox May Have Backfired
entry.summary: Ships arriving in Australia may have had scabs from survivors intended as a preventative

entry.title: Cluster of extremely rare cancer cases suddenly strikes kids in wealthy SoCal city
entry.summary: The families say they have no proof of what may be behind the diagnoses but are urging officials to examine whether pesticides used throughout the community could be a factor.

entry.title: Cyclospora, a stomach-churning parasite, has reached Mass. Here’s what to know.
entry.summary: Patients are experiencing chronic diarrhea as health officials scramble to find the source. Massachusetts has become one of more than a dozen states to report cases since May 1.

entry.title: Major breakthrough for cancer treatment
entry.summary: Plus: CTA crime drops, Taste of Chicago must-eats and more.

entry.title: Strength Training These 3 Muscles Will Lead to the Biggest Heart Health Benefits, Doctor Says
entry.summary: Strength training bigger muscles can give your heart a workout, says Dr. Joseph Herrera, a sports medicine expert. Learn more about which muscle groups are the best to train.

entry.title: Column | What a sleep expert eats in a day for better rest at night
entry.summary: This professor’s research has found that certain foods are associated with deep, more restorative sleep.

entry.title: 90 percent of U.S. adults have this syndrome — but most have never heard of it
entry.summary: New guidelines may soon make the disorder known as CKM syndrome a common diagnosis.

entry.title: An Illegal Injectable Tanning Drug Is Trending on TikTok – And It's Giving People Dangerous Moles
entry.summary: Dermatologists in multiple countries are expressing concern about TikTok influencers promoting what is known by some as 'the Barbie drug'.

entry.title: ﻿Iowa's first measles case of 2026 reflects a changing reality for doctors, health officials
entry.summary: Iowa's first measles case of 2026 is highlighting how rising cases across the U.S. have Iowa doctors preparing for a disease once thought to be on its way to extinction.

entry.title: Tiny silica particles wiped out aggressive prostate cancer in mice
entry.summary: Tiny silica nanoparticles engineered to seek out prostate cancer caused tumor cells to self-destruct and supercharged the immune system in preclinical mouse studies. Combined with immunotherapy, the treatment produced complete remissions in multiple mice, rai…

entry.title: New York City health officials search for source of Upper East Side Legionnaires' outbreak linked to 36 cases
entry.summary: Officials say they plan to release the addresses of any building whose cooling towers test positive during the initial screening process.

entry.title: Ladera Ranch families concerned after several children diagnosed with rare cancer
entry.summary: Families in Ladera Ranch say they want answers as to why several children in their one community have all been diagnosed with the same rare cancer.

entry.title: UK girl, 12, underwent six grueling –and unnecessary — rounds of chemo after misdiagnosis
entry.summary: “Those doctors ruined my little girl’s whole childhood. I feel so let down by everybody,” the girl’s mom said.

entry.title: Norton warns parasitic infection causing 'explosive' diarrhea is on the rise in Kentucky
entry.summary: The parasite causing "explosive diarrhea" across the country has made its way to Kentuckiana.

entry.title: Rare transplant gives Stage 4 lung cancer patients a second chance at life — a Capitol Hill man is proof
entry.summary: A D.C. cancer survivor told WTOP a double lung transplant performed in Chicago saved his life.

entry.title: Sexually transmitted diarrhoea spreading rapidly among gay men in UK
entry.summary: Variants of Shigella are causing more disease and growing more antibiotic-resistant, a new study from the University of Cambridge has found.

entry.title: Alcohol Alters Brain Flexibility Based on Hidden Dementia Markings
entry.summary: A new study proves alcohol interacts differently with amyloid-beta and tau pathologies in Alzheimer's models.

entry.title: Scientists Just Connected This Daily Habit to Higher Blood Pressure
entry.summary: Sugar-sweetened beverages and fruit juice intake may raise blood pressure risk, according to a new study. Learn healthy swaps to support your heart.

entry.title: I'm a Longevity Doctor. Bryan Johnson's Diagnosis Reminds Us That Longevity Isn't About Perfection.
entry.summary: Bryan Johnson, the "longevity guru," has spent more than $2 million on a quest to stay young. This week, he disclosed that he has an autoimmune disease that causes the stomach to eat itself. As a practicing physician who helps patients live longer, believe me…

entry.title: MenB vaccine offers men no protection from gonorrhoea, claims major study
entry.summary: The Meningitis B vaccine does not prevent gonorrhoea infections despite being offered to at-risk men, scientists say.

entry.title: Scientists Discover a Potential Driver of Chronic Fatigue Syndrome Hiding Deep in The Brain
entry.summary: Our understanding of the human brain is experiencing a watershed moment.

entry.title: West Nile virus detected in mosquitoes from 13 Orange County cities
entry.summary: West Nile virus has now been detected in mosquito samples from 13 Orange County cities, with Fullerton accounting for the largest share of positive samples as officials prepare to spray parts of the city this week. According to the Orange County Mosquito and …

entry.title: Fish Oil Pills May Not Boost Brain Health After All
entry.summary: Research finds pills don't improve cognition or prevent dementia

entry.title: Oncologist Reveals The Five Cancer Symptoms People Most Commonly Dismiss As Normal Aging Or Stress
entry.summary: “The goal isn’t to make people anxious but to make them aware that getting older should not automatically be used to explain away changes in your health,” the doctor said.

entry.title: Health officials confirm measles case in central Iowa
entry.summary: A case of measles has been confirmed in an adult Polk County resident.

entry.title: GLP-1 Users Say Ozempic Feet Side Effect Is Hard to Ignore
entry.summary: It may leave patients looking for new sneakers.

entry.title: My Sister-in-Law Is Going to Permanently Disable Her Child. She Won’t Heed My Warnings.
entry.summary: I'm so worried.

entry.title: Cancer cases worldwide are expected to soar in the coming decades, a report finds. Here’s why.
entry.summary: The World Health Organization tempered optimism about improvements in cancer treatment and said global health care inequities are driving more cases and deaths.

entry.title: Why a gene-editing breakthrough has some experts worried
entry.summary: New research shows it’s possible to edit the DNA of human embryos with more precision. But scientists warn it’s still not safe.

entry.title: Bryan Johnson’s chronic disease is notoriously difficult to diagnose
entry.summary: Why longevity entrepreneur Bryan Johnson’s chronic disease is notoriously difficult to diagnose.

entry.title: Chromatin landscape and epigenetic heterogeneity of acute myeloid leukaemia
entry.summary: An ATAC-seq-based approach is used to classify acute myeloid leukaemia (AML) into 16 epigenomic subgroups, and provides insight into the role of non-genetic mechanisms in determining pathogenesis, clinical behaviour and drug sensitivity in this disease.

entry.title: Oxygen relief for a proteostasis-driven disease associated with mitochondrial complex I dysfunction
entry.summary: Hypoxia has emerged as an unexpected therapy for some mitochondrial diseases. Garg et al. now extend this concept beyond primary respiratory chain mutations, showing that reducing oxygen tension can rescue a disease of mitochondrial protein quality control li…

entry.title: Hypoxia rescues complex 1-associated disease caused by proteostatic defects
entry.summary: The HTRA2–CLPB proteostasis system is a mitochondrial quality control module whose dysfunction drives local oxygen toxicity, which can be mitigated by lowering ambient oxygen levels.

entry.title: We Asked a Doctor Whether Daily Creatine Is Safe Long Term
entry.summary: Some people take creatine to enhance their workouts, but is it safe to take this supplement long term?

entry.title: Teen’s ‘dehydration’ headaches turned out to something much more dangerous
entry.summary: A teen put her headaches down to dehydration during a heatwave, but they were a sign of a ruptured tumor.

entry.title: 'Serious illness': Ohio warns of cyclosporiasis outbreak causing explosive diarrhea
entry.summary: Ohio health officials are urging residents to take extra precautions when handling fruits and vegetables to help prevent a disease that can lead to watery, and sometimes explosive, diarrhea.

entry.title: Diet–microbiome synergy underlies obesity-associated immunotherapy efficacy
entry.summary: Diet shapes obesity-associated therapeutic responses to immune checkpoint inhibitors through gut microbial metabolism and host anti-tumour immunity, demonstrated in mouse custom-diet models and human-to-mouse fecal microbiota transplantation ex…

entry.title: Are you getting the right cholesterol test? Millions may not be, study says
entry.summary: Millions of Americans get their LDL, or "bad" cholesterol, tested. But another test may do a better job of finding who needs treatment.

entry.title: New research suggests brain health can be measurably improved at any age
entry.summary: Can you strengthen your brain health as you get older? New research shows that adults of all ages who engage in brief, daily cognitive exercises can significantly improve their mental clarity, social connectedness, and emotional resilience over time.

entry.title: Millions of mosquitos will be released in this Sacramento neighborhood. Here’s why
entry.summary: The Sacramento-Yolo district released 144,000 sterile males in Rosemont as part of a 2.3 million mosquito Wolbachia program to curb Aedes aegypti and protect public health.

entry.title: Healthy Returns: Employers aren't expanding coverage of GLP-1 obesity drugs – many are finding ways around it
entry.summary: Around 36% of employers said they provide coverage of GLP-1s for both diabetes and weight loss, the same as 2025 and up from 34% in 2024, the survey said.

entry.title: The One Nut Dietitians Recommend for Brain, Heart, and Gut Health
entry.summary: Walnuts may be the best nut for brain, heart, and gut health, thanks to omega-3s, fiber, antioxidants, and key minerals.

entry.title: Cancer cases worldwide will soar in the coming decades, a report finds. Here’s why.
entry.summary: Around 20.6 million people were diagnosed with cancer in 2024.

entry.title: Smelling chocolate could make your leg day workout easier, even on an empty stomach
entry.summary: Smelling dark chocolate before exercise helped men on an empty stomach complete more reps without feeling like the workout was harder.

entry.title: The ‘Big Sign’ of Melanoma and Skin Cancer Symptoms a Dermatologist Monitors in Herself
entry.summary: A dermatologist reveals the ‘big sign’ a mole may be melanoma and the skin cancer warning signs she looks for in her own body.
